headline: All majors below the 200-day line, longs crowded and liquidating, whales moving Bitcoin to exchanges in a falling market.
paragraphs: The regime is clear and uniform: every major asset in the input (BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, BNB, DOGE, ADA) trades below its 200-day simple moving average, and not one has printed a golden cross. Bitcoin sits 48.6% below its 12-month high at 64,105. Ethereum is down 61.4% from its peak. Solana, the most battered of the names here, has fallen 70.1% from its yearly top. This is not a market resting above structural support; this is a market that has not reclaimed its intermediate uptrend. The total market cap dropped 1.08% in the last 24 hours to 2.28 trillion, and Bitcoin dominance holds steady at 56.5%, meaning the rot is even across the board, not concentrated in alts.

Momentum, however, is not syncing cleanly with that downtrend. Bitcoin's RSI sits at 49.6, Ethereum at 54.7, and both have MACD above the signal line, a bullish technical posture in a still-falling market. That divergence (upside momentum against downside price and long-term trend) is a tension to track, but it has not yet resolved into a reclaim of the 200-day line. Solana is different: its RSI of 43.2 is weak and MACD is below signal, confirming the downtrend. XRP and BNB also show MACD above signal despite price weakness, a pattern consistent across the major longs. Doge, meanwhile, is deeply oversold at RSI 31.7, barely moving at 0% over 24 hours, and ADA is the volatility outlier at 70.6% annualized realized vol, suggesting forced liquidations or thin order book liquidity.

Positioning reveals the real stress. Bitcoin funding is elevated: 0.46% over 8 hours annualizes to 5.1%, and the long-to-short ratio on OKX perpetuals stands at 1.54, meaning longs outnumber shorts by more than half. In the last 24 hours, 1,599 Bitcoin positions liquidated, with longs wiped for 15.5 million against shorts blown out for 3.0 million. That 5-to-1 skew toward long liquidations in a falling market is the tape's loudest signal: the market is shaking out leveraged buyers. Ethereum shows the same pattern, 1.48 longs per short, 1,600 liquidations in 20 hours, and 11.6 million in long liquidations to 4.8 million in short liquidations. Solana is worse: 2.4 longs per short, 2.8 million in long liquidations to just 78k in short liquidations. The derivatives market is crowded on the long side and bleeding when price falls. This is fragility, not strength.

Whale behavior deepens the picture. In the last 24 hours, 237 million dollars in Bitcoin flowed onto exchanges, with the biggest moves going to OKex (171.4 million) and Binance (65.6 million). That is not accumulation into cold storage; that is selling pressure at the margin. Simultaneously, 118.5 million in Tether also moved onto OKex, suggesting whales are posting stablecoin dry powder, ready to catch a falling knife. The weekly flow trend is the kicker: the last four weeks show 1.55 billion, 1.42 billion, then minus 135 million, then minus 346 million. Whales have been net sellers into the draw-down, not buyers. The Fear and Greed index sits at 28, labeled Fear, with a 30-day range of 11 to 33, meaning the crowd is scared and has been scared. Yet the crowd's fear and the whales' selling pressure are more aligned than divergent here, a rare moment when sentiment and positioning agree.

ETF flows offer one counterweight: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a 225.1 million dollar net outflow on July 23, but the cumulative total is still 51.7 billion inflow year-to-date. Ethereum ETFs took in 26.3 million on the same day and sit 11.3 billion cumulative. This is slower than earlier in the cycle, but the traditional finance bid has not reversed. Stablecoin float sits at 306 billion, down 1.98% in 30 days, a modest drawdown that suggests not all dry powder has been deployed. Network difficulty on Bitcoin is set to retarget in 178 blocks (down 0.4% on the last adjustment), and fees remain minimal at 2 satoshis per byte for fastest confirmation, indicating no network congestion.

Watch three conditions: whether Bitcoin reclaims the 200-day line (a regime shift if it holds), whether the 8-hour funding on BTC or ETH flips negative (a sign the long crowding has broken), and whether the weekly whale flows reverse back to positive (accumulation re-entering the market). Until those move, the tape reads as a prolonged drawdown in a bear-regime market, with longs being systematically cleared out and whales gradually raising cash. No reversal signal has fired yet.
